current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: The Etowah River is currently very warm and presents tough trout fishing conditions. Recent stocking improves odds using stocked-trout flies despite the warm water.
generated_text.best_window: The day 1 morning is the best window because moderate sky cover and light winds provide balanced conditions without strong pressure changes, ideal for a balanced nymph approach targeting stocked trout.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports water temperature at 79.7°F, flow at 97.7 cfs, gage height 3.69 ft, and turbidity 8.6 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current sky cover averages 22%, pressure is stable at 30.05 inHg, wind averages 4.5 mph with gusts up to 13.8 mph, and rain chance is low at 15% with no significant rainfall expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely positioned near cold-water refuges, oxygenated water, runs, seams, and structure, focusing on comfortable feeding lanes near cover due to warm water stress. Present clean drifts with smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worm, and duracell nymphs. Exercise caution as warm water may increase trout stress.
